title: diosma odorata
intro: Diosma odorata, also known as the Cape buchu, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to South Africa, belonging to the Rutaceae family. Diosma odorata has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is rich in essential oils, particularly borneol, camphor, and 1,8-cineole, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and promoting relaxation. Diosma odorata is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions.
